title: Deploy Workload
content:
# Deploy Workload When you deploy a workload, you can choose whether to deploy immediately or to stage the workload at the edge and apply it at a later time. | Field | Option | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | Standard | None | This will use the standard Kubernetes deployment process for workload containers and the workload will be automatically started after downloaded to the edge. | | Staged | Stage Only | This will download the workload to the edge but will not start the workload. You will need to send an additional command to start the workload either through the Kelvin API or locally in the terminal of the edge computer | | Staged | Instant Apply | This will use Kelvin's deployment process for workload containers and the workload will be automatically started after downloaded to the edge.

The difference to the Kubernetes deployment process is that Kelvin will download all workloads and stage them first. Only when all workloads have been successfully downloaded will the start command be given to all the workloads. | ## Default Settings To simplify the deployment process, you can set default settings that will be automatically chosen when deploying. The default settings can overwritten during each deploy if special conditions are required. When completing the deployment of a Workload there is an option to update the default settings with the current deploy selections. ## Deploy Standard Workload On this page we will show you how to deploy a `staged` workload. There are different options available depending on the `type` of SmartApp that is being deployed.
=== "Kelvin SmartApp™" To start, go to **Applications**, select a Kelvin SmartApp™ with an App icon { width=20 height=20 }, click on the **Workloads** tab then click on the **Deploy Workload** button; Select the Kelvin SmartApp™ App version, then click **Next** to go to Step 2. In Step 2 select the Assets you want to add to the Kelvin SmartApp™ then click the **Next** button. Assets that are already running this Application will not be shown. In Step 3 you have a range of options available. You will only be able to deploy your workload once all options have a green check {width="10"}. **Parameters** Select the Parameter values that each Asset should start using for this Kelvin SmartApp™ when deployed. When there are many Assets or Asset Parameters to set, you can download a blank csv file with the headers preset with the parameter names. You only need to add the Assets names and the parameter values you want to overwrite the default values for. Any blank spaces will be automatically filled with the default Asset Parameter values. **I/O Mapping** Link the Asset's Data Streams to the Kelvin SmartApp™ input/output variables. Asset's Data Streams are declared as pairs in any Connection that is deployed. Kelvin SmartApp™ input/output variables are defined by the Developers in the `app.yaml` file. Aggregated Mapping lets you select all possible Data Stream names for an Application Input or Output. The system will intelligently determine the correct name to use for each Asset. You can select multiple Data Streams for each Application Input or Output. When you click the Deploy button, the system will intelligently check each selected Asset and its Data Streams to find matching Asset/Data Stream pairs that have been declared and deployed on a Connection, and will then use those pairs for the Application Input or Output. This allows you to add many Assets at once, even if each Asset uses a different Data Stream name for the same Application Input. Advanced Mapping allows you to manually select the Asset / Data Stream pairs individually for each Asset. **Actions** This will list all actions that will be triggered for the new Assets. Actions are triggers that send information from a publisher application to a consumer application. Consumer applications centralize common tasks, such as sending emails, so developers don't need to reimplement them for every new application. You can understand more about actions [in the concepts section here](../../../overview/concepts/custom-action.md). Each Asset can only be assigned to a specific trigger in a single Application on the Kelvin Platform. If the trigger is already in use by another Application for the same Asset, this configuration is not allowed and a warning will be shown. **Configuration** Add any configuration information that is required by the Kelvin SmartApp™. **System** Here you will be able to tune all the system variables that are present in the `app.yaml` file of the Application. You can choose to edit it using a UI or directly on the YAML or JSON. Using YAML or JSON is very useful for fast application of settings if you have preset configurations and want to copy/paste them into here. *Env Vars* Here will be shown the list of default Environment Variables and the default values that were set in the `app.yaml` file of the Application during development. You can edit these and change both the environment name or value. You can also add addition Environment variables. *Resources* Apply resources available for the Workload. See [Set CPU/Memory Limits for detailed documentation](../advanced/set-cpu-and-memory-limits.md) on this section. *Ports* Open ports to your Workload. *Volumes* Mount volumes to your Workload. *Advanced* Choose if you would like to add `Privileged` mode to the Workload Optionally choose a health check service for your Workload. These are Kubernetes liveness probes and detailed information can be found on the [Kubernetes Website](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/). If you use a Health check option, make sure you add code to respond to the liveness probe requests. **Cluster** Choose a cluster to run your application on. **Deployment** | Field | Option | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | Staged | Stage Only | This will download the workload to the edge but will not start the workload. You will need to send an additional command to start the workload either through the Kelvin API or locally in the terminal of the edge computer | | Staged | Instant Apply | This will use Kelvin's deployment process for workload containers and the workload will be automatically started after downloaded to the edge.

The difference to the Kubernetes deployment process is that Kelvin will download all workloads and stage them first. Only when all workloads have been successfully downloaded will the start command be given to all the workloads. | **Asset-Workload Distribution** : Define how many Assets are controlled by each Workload that is deployed. Allowing each Workload to handle more Assets is more efficient in your edge hardware memory and runtime resource usage. Be aware that when deploying a workload, checks are made on sufficient memory available and the deploy will fail if there is not enough memory. However no runtime resource usage is calculated at deployment, so excessive asset allocation can impact SmartApp performance due to CPU and memory constraints. When you are ready and you have a green check {width="10"} on all options, you will see the **Staged** button turn blue. Click on it and the workload will be staged according to the type of staged deployment to perform. You can even deploy the app if the Cluster is offline. It will be placed in a queue with a status of Pending and will be automatically deployed when the Cluster comes back online. === "Docker Application" To start, go to **Applications**, select an application with the Docker Apps tag { width=20 height=20 }, click on the **Workloads** tab then click on the **Deploy Workload** button; Select the Docker App version and give it a name. When ready click **Next** to go to Step 2. The ID will be filled in automatically when you type a name. Normally you can accept the default, though you have the option to create you own custom name ID. In Step 2 you have a range of options available. You will only be able to deploy your workload once all options have a green check {width="10"}. **Configuration** Add any configuration information that is required by the Docker App. **System** Here you will be able to tune all the system variables that are present in the `app.yaml` file of the Docker App. You can choose to edit it using a UI or directly on the YAML or JSON. Using YAML or JSON is very useful for fast application of settings if you have preset configurations and want to copy/paste them into here. *Env Vars* Here will be shown the list of default Environment Variables and the default values that were set in the `app.yaml` file of the Docker App during development. You can edit these and change both the environment name or value. You can also add addition Environment variables. *Resources* Apply resources available for the Workload. See [Set CPU/Memory Limits for detailed documentation](../advanced/set-cpu-and-memory-limits.md) on this section. *Ports* Open ports to your Workload. *Volumes* Mount volumes to your Workload. *Advanced* Choose if you would like to add `Privileged` mode to the Workload; Optionally choose a health check service for your Workload. These are Kubernetes liveness probes and detailed information can be found on the [Kubernetes Website](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/). If you use a Health check option, make sure you add code to respond to the liveness probe requests. **Cluster** Choose a cluster to run your Docker App on. **Deployment** | Field | Option | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | Staged | Stage Only | This will download the workload to the edge but will not start the workload. You will need to send an additional command to start the workload either through the Kelvin API or locally in the terminal of the edge computer | | Staged | Instant Apply | This will use Kelvin's deployment process for workload containers and the workload will be automatically started after downloaded to the edge.

The difference to the Kubernetes deployment process is that Kelvin will download all workloads and stage them first. Only when all workloads have been successfully downloaded will the start command be given to all the workloads. | When you are ready and all mandatory areas are filled in, you will see the **Stage** button turn blue. Click on it and the workload will be staged according to the type of staged deployment to perform. You can even [deploy the app if the Cluster is offline](./deploy-workloads-deploy-offline.md). It will be placed in a queue with a status of Pending and will be automatically deployed when the Cluster comes back online.
